user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: HSBC Q1 IWPB: Wealth Fees +15%, $34bn Asia NNIA — Execution Bar Rises for APAC Teams
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's Q1 IWPB results validate the Asia wealth pivot and directly raise the execution bar for Adam's change teams, who must now sustain the operational capacity to convert accelerating inflows into incremental revenue.
bullets: HSBC IWPB revenue reached $3.75bn (+1.5% YoY) in Q1 2026, with wealth fees growing 15% — driven by investment distribution (+21%), insurance (+19%), and private banking (+8%). Group RoTE of 17.3% signals strategy-at-scale delivery.

$34bn of the group's $39bn Q1 NNIA originated in Asia — confirming APAC as the growth engine and demanding that Adam's WPB change programmes are sized and sequenced to match this velocity heading into H2 2026.

Group insurance income grew 49% and pre-tax profit held at $1.23bn despite 5% cost growth — the discipline benchmark Adam's change portfolio must protect as new Wealth Powerhouse integration demands increase.

source_links.title: WealthBriefing: HSBC Q1 IWPB Revenue Analysis

talking_points.headline: Lloyds Deploys Agentic AI to 21M Accounts — HSBC Now Has a Benchmark to Beat
talking_points.why_it_matters: Lloyds' enterprise-wide agentic AI rollout sets the first concrete peer benchmark for Adam's AI-enabled change agenda in APAC, defining what scaled deployment looks like in a comparable universal bank.
bullets: Lloyds Banking Group deployed its Envoy agentic AI platform across 21 million customer accounts — orchestrating autonomous workflows across customer service, risk management, operations, and engineering. The bank targets £100m in AI value in 2026 vs £50m delivered in 2025.

Industry data confirms only 35% of wealth advisors actively use AI tools (10.5% daily) — the implementation gap is widening, and banks systematising AI into advice workflows are building compounding competitive advantage now.

With Lloyds providing a public ROI framework (£50m → £100m), Adam's change team can reference this when building HSBC APAC's AI deployment business case and defining success metrics for the WPB AI programme.

source_links.title: FinTech Mag: Lloyds Agentic AI 21M Accounts
Fintech Global: AI Wealth Management Gap

talking_points.headline: OCBC Q1 Wealth Record S$342bn + HSBC Indonesia Integration Now Live — Clock Ticking
talking_points.why_it_matters: OCBC's record Q1 results and active integration of HSBC's Indonesia wealth business set both a competitive performance benchmark and a hard operational deadline for HSBC's own change execution programme.
bullets: OCBC's wealth management AUM hit a record S$342bn (US$271bn) in Q1 2026, up 12% YoY, with fee income +34% and overall wealth income +50% — demonstrating the compound leverage from sustained execution on a multi-year change programme.

OCBC is actively executing its HSBC Indonesia wealth acquisition (1,300 staff, ~S$6.6bn AUM) alongside hiring 30-50 new wealth bankers in Hong Kong — creating direct capacity and talent pressure on HSBC WPB in Adam's core markets.
DBS Private Bank simultaneously won Euromoney's World's Best for Digital Assets 2026, reinforcing that HSBC's top APAC competitors are scaling AUM records and innovation awards in parallel.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Q1 2026 IWPB: Wealth Fees +15% YoY, Revenue Reaches $3.75bn
HSBC News.summary: HSBC's International Wealth and Premier Banking revenues rose 1.5% YoY to $3.75bn in Q1 2026. Wealth fees and other income grew 15% driven by insurance (+19%), investment distribution (+21%), and private banking (+8%). Pre-tax profit held stable at $1.23bn while operating expenses grew 5%.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Q1 2026 Group Results: $9.4bn Profit, 17.3% RoTE, $39bn NNIA
HSBC News.summary: HSBC posted $9.4bn group pre-tax profit for Q1 2026 with a 17.3% RoTE. Revenue rose 4% driven by wealth fees (+20%) and FX income. Insurance income grew 49%, investment distribution revenues rose 21%. The group attracted $39bn in net new money in Q1, of which $34bn came from Asia, reinforcing the Asia-wealth pivot.

AI & Technology.title: AI in Wealth Management: Closing the 65% Implementation Gap
AI & Technology.summary: Only 35% of financial advisory intermediaries actively use AI tools, with just 10.5% doing so daily. The analysis identifies data governance, talent, and change management as the binding constraints. Banks that systematize AI into advice workflows achieve repeatable, auditable, scalable advisory at lower cost — the decisive differentiator in 2026 as competitors accelerate deployment.

Competitor Intelligence.title: OCBC Q1 2026: Wealth AUM Hits Record S$342bn, Fee Income +34% YoY
Competitor Intelligence.summary: OCBC's Q1 2026 results show wealth management AUM reaching a record S$342bn (US$271bn), up 12% YoY, with fee income rising 34% to S$422m. Group net profit grew 5% to S$1.97bn. OCBC continues to integrate HSBC's Indonesia wealth business (1,300 staff, S$6.6bn AUM) while hiring 30-50 wealth bankers in Hong Kong under its Next Frontier strategy.

Competitor Intelligence.title: OCBC Q1 2026: Net Profit +5%, Wealth Income +50%, Next Frontier Executing
Competitor Intelligence.summary: OCBC reported Q1 2026 net profit of S$1.97bn (+5% YoY) with wealth management income rising 50% from a year earlier. Corporate wealth income more than tripled. OCBC is executing its Next Frontier strategy, strengthening its Singapore and Hong Kong hubs while completing its S$2.5bn capital return plan and integrating the HSBC Indonesia wealth acquisition.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Deloitte APAC Q1 2026 Regulatory Update: Governance Top Priority
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Deloitte's Q1 2026 APAC regulatory update identifies governance and accountability as the dominant theme. Key developments include Japan finalizing cyber risk preparedness guidelines, Indonesia mandating IT governance for banks, MAS consulting on comprehensive AI Risk Management Guidelines (covering explainability and lifecycle controls), and Hong Kong launching Phase 2A of its Sustainable Finance Taxonomy.
